messages.content:
Eres un experto en el codebase de ProjectSight (Trimble Construction).
El proyecto usa .NET/C# en el backend con ASP.NET MVC/Razor y Angular en el frontend.

Reglas críticas del proyecto (nunca las violes):
- Backend: Nunca uses ORM (Entity Framework, Dapper). Solo IDBHelper con stored procedures por nombre.
- Backend: Toda operación de BD va a través de stored procedures nombrados. Jamás inline SQL.
- Frontend: Nunca uses Angular HttpClient. Usa RxJS ajax() via APIBase / APIFactory.
- Frontend: Usa DestroyRef + subscribeTo() para subscriptions, NO Subscription arrays manuales.
- Frontend: Nunca hardcodees colores hex. Usa variables CSS de Default.css.
- No edites delta_procs.sql directamente; se genera automáticamente desde base_procs.sql.
- Agregar o quitar [UseInAPI] en una propiedad dispara APIClientComponentGenerator al compilar.

Cuando implementes cambios, sigue el patrón exacto del diff que se te proporciona.

# PR #10894: PS-69003 PotentialChangeValue will be updated when a SCO linked to a contract is deleted and the PCO items was already linked to the contract.
**Tipo:** unknown
**Branch:** `wip/abdul/PS-69003` → `feature/Improv`
**Labels:** CFD

## Descripción
PS-69003 PotentialChangeValue will be updated when a SCO linked to a contract is deleted and the PCO items was already linked to the contract.

## Feedback de reviewers
[COMMENTED por @copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]]: ## Pull request overview

This PR updates SQL procedure logic for PS-69003 so contract `PotentialChangeValue` is restored when an SCO/PCO item link is deleted, keeping contract potential values aligned with direct PCO item linkage.

**Changes:**
- Adds `PotentialChangeValue` adjustment when deleting `SubContractCOPCOItemLinks`.
- Regenerates `delta_procs.sql` and updates the stored procedure hash.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `Core/SQL/base_procs.sql` | Adds the contract `PotentialChangeValue` update in the canonical stored procedure. |
| `Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql` | Mirrors the stored procedure change for database upgrades, with regenerated refresh-module ordering. |
| `Core/SQL/record_procs_hash.sql` | Updates the procedure hash after SQL regeneration. |
[COMMENTED por @copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]]: ## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
[Comentario en Core/SQL/base_procs.sql:53814 por @Copilot]: This update changes `PotentialChangeValue`, but the contract history entry built below still records only `PotentialValue`. Other contract-value paths that update `PotentialChangeValue` include it in the history XML (for example, `s_enc_add_or_update_SubContractCOPCOItemLinks` and direct PCO item delete), so this path will leave the contract audit history missing the restored potential-change amount. Please capture the old `PotentialChangeValue` and add a corresponding `PotentialChangeValue` history row here as well.
[Comentario en Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql:3151 por @Copilot]: This mirrored procedure update also needs the history XML to include `PotentialChangeValue`; otherwise upgraded databases will update the value but the generated contract history will show only the `PotentialValue` change. Please regenerate/update this block after adding the `PotentialChangeValue` history entry in `base_procs.sql`.
[Comentario en Core/SQL/base_procs.sql:53814 por @Copilot]: This is the behavior fixed by PS-69003, but there is no regression coverage for deleting an SCO after its PCO item has been direct-linked to the contract and verifying `PotentialChangeValue` is restored. The existing add-link test asserts the inverse transition, so adding a delete-side assertion/test would prevent this accounting path from regressing again.
[Comentario en Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql:3042 por @Copilot]: Remove the inline "-- wrong order" note from the parameter list. It’s not actionable for callers and makes the generated script look like it contains known defects; if parameter ordering truly matters somewhere, the fix should be to reorder the parameters (and any corresponding call-site docs) rather than leaving a warning comment in the signature.
[Comentario en Core/SQL/base_procs.sql:53814 por @Copilot]: This procedure performs multiple writes across PotentialCOItems, History, and Contracts without an explicit transaction. In autocommit mode, a runtime failure after the first UPDATE/INSERT can leave partially-applied changes; `SET XACT_ABORT ON` only guarantees rollback of the *current* statement unless the statements are wrapped in a transaction. Consider wrapping the write section (after validation/early returns) in a single `BEGIN TRAN`/`COMMIT` so the unlink + history + contract adjustments are atomic.
[Comentario en Core/SQL/base_procs.sql:53814 por @Copilot]: The new Contract `PotentialChangeValue` adjustment on SCO link removal looks correct, but there doesn’t appear to be an integration test covering the specific scenario described in the PR (PCO item was already directly linked to a contract, then linked to an SCO for that contract, then the SCO is deleted). Consider adding a test alongside the existing SubcontractCO delete/link tests (e.g., `Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/SubContractCOs/BusinessRulesTests.cs`) that deletes the SCO and asserts the contract’s `PotentialChangeValue`/`PotentialValue` are restored as expected.

## Código ANTES del cambio
### Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
```sql
﻿USE ProjectSight
GO
SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
GO
 /* Verify the database version */
DECLARE
  @MinimumVersion BIGINT ,
  @CurrentVersion BIGINT

SELECT @CurrentVersion = major * 1000000 + minor * 30000 + revision
  FROM versions
 WHERE program_id = 'Core DB'

SET @MinimumVersion = 10000000

IF ISNULL(@CurrentVersion, 0) < @MinimumVersion
  BEGIN
    DECLARE
      @DatabaseName nvarchar(100) ,
      @MinVersionString nvarchar(20)

    SET @DatabaseName = DB_NAME()
    SET @MinVersionString = CAST(@MinimumVersion AS nvarchar)

    RAISERROR('The database %s is required to be version %s or greater.', 16, 1, @DatabaseName, @MinVersionString)
    RETURN
  END
GO

IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM versions WHERE program_id = 'Procs')
  INSERT INTO versions (program_id, major, minor, revision) VALUES ('Procs', 0, 0, 0)
GO

IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM versions WHERE program_id = 'Previous')
  INSERT INTO versions (program_id, major, minor, revision) VALUES ('Previous', 0, 0, 0)
GO
 
/***************

[... truncado 3097629 chars ...]

upListID ,
         FullPath AS UDFLookup_Description
    FROM Locations
GO

/****** Object:  View v_enc_LookupLists_for_UDFs ******/
CREATE VIEW dbo.v_enc_LookupLists_for_UDFs
AS
   /* This view is only used to simplify list and report data retrieval for UDFs. It basically masks the other columns in LookupLists that can clash with fields. */

    /* Need to generate names that don't clash with those in DEF tables or functions */
  SELECT LookupListID AS UDFLookup_LookupListID ,
         Description AS UDFLookup_Description
    FROM LookupLists
GO

SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF
GO
/*********************Do not make any changes below this section.  -  Start ***************************/

-- DB VERSION NUMBER
-- ## UPDATE versions set major = $major, minor = $minor, revision = 0 where program_id = 'Procs'
UPDATE versions set major = 10, minor = 183, revision = 0 where program_id = 'Procs'

/*********************Do not make any changes below this section.  -  End  ****************************/

```

### Core/SQL/record_procs_hash.sql
```sql
﻿UPDATE versions SET md5 = 'AEE821794ED1B577F0D325A9E8B56BEE' WHERE program_id = 'Procs';
UPDATE versions SET md5 = '3624C59E546932A316671F0ED98ED205' WHERE program_id = 'Previous';

WITH Hashs AS (SELECT * FROM (VALUES (10, 172, 'F891D4A5A41A1014B91F9CB48EDED7AD'),
(10, 173, '20C2357832E145D44816BDB66B45D82B'),
(10, 174, 'D86A1C4B49ADE0F49A8938285908EDBC'),
(10, 175, '7620D3F6A21337BB1B148D15CC9193BA'),
(10, 176, 'D54B39BE56D64A404CA370BF367BEDA1'),
(10, 177, 'C63313859E594971DCDAAADE89D66965'),
(10, 178, 'EE8AE5A47C0090C0D756F3E224553631'),
(10, 179, '2EA8A2DB2278D65B65B16A1826E3AFC5'),
(10, 180, '0DEEFAA3C86BF640A749E81404023358'),
(10, 181, 'E6277242468E8A3A15FFD969F77DE4F1'),
(10, 182, '6194A3F4756931A2F80DB194D813B04F'),
(10, 183, 'B0858F23C11D5E24C01853C8429E90E6')) AS Hashs(Major, Minor, Hash))
UPDATE v 
   SET md5 = (SELECT Hash FROM Hashs h WHERE h.Major = v.Major AND h.Minor = v.Minor) 
  FROM versions v 
 WHERE program_id = 'Core DB'

```

Genera el diff exacto que implementa los cambios descritos.

diff --git a/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql b/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
index d6514d5751..f8670bd1b1 100644
--- a/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
+++ b/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
@@ -53802,6 +53802,7 @@ DECLARE
     BEGIN
       DECLARE
         @PotentialValue    money ,
+        @PotentialChangeValue money ,
         @ItemKey           nvarchar(100) ,
         @HistoryEntry      NVARCHAR(MAX) ,
         @ModifiedRecords   ProjectIDRecordIDGUIDTableType
@@ -53809,6 +53810,8 @@ DECLARE
       UPDATE Contracts
          SET @PotentialValue      = PotentialValue ,
              PotentialValue       = PotentialValue - @CostAppliedAmount ,
+             @PotentialChangeValue= PotentialChangeValue,
+             PotentialChangeValue = PotentialChangeValue + @CostAppliedAmount,
              LastModified         = @WhenRequested ,
              LastModifiedBy       = @RequestedBy ,
              LastModifiedByUserID = @RequestedByUserID
@@ -53817,12 +53820,19 @@ DECLARE
              INSERTED.GUID
              INTO @ModifiedRecords
        WHERE ContractID = @ContractID
-         AND Deleted = 0
+         AND Deleted = 0;
 
-      SET @HistoryEntry = (SELECT Name = CAST('PotentialValue' AS NVARCHAR(100)),
-                                  NewValue = @PotentialValue - @CostAppliedAmount ,
-                                  OldValue = @PotentialValue
-                           FOR XML RAW)
+         WITH HistoryEntries AS
+                    (SELECT Name = CAST('PotentialChangeValue' AS NVARCHAR(100)),
+                            NewValue = @PotentialChangeValue + @CostAppliedAmount ,
+                            OldValue = @PotentialChangeValue
+
+                     UNION
+
+                     SELECT Name = CAST('PotentialValue' AS NVARCHAR(100)),
+                            NewValue = @PotentialValue - @CostAppliedAmount ,
+                            OldValue = @PotentialValue)
+         SELECT @HistoryEntry = (SELECT * FROM HistoryEntries FOR XML RAW)
 
       SELECT @ItemKey = PCONumber + '-' + @ItemNumber
         FROM PotentialCO
